senses.definition: to combine something with another substance or element

examples.x: The story became more engaging by blending in elements seamlessly.
Music production requires expertise in blending in sounds for a mesmerizing track.
The chef is skilled at blending in flavors to create unique dishes.
Blending in ideas during a brainstorming session often leads to innovative solutions.

senses.definition: to make something a permanent and necessary part of a larger system or structure

examples.x: The developer will build in automatic updates to keep the software current.
The team plans to build in accessibility features for a wider user base.
The company decided to build in quality control measures for every production stage.
The company strives to build in quality control measures from the start.

senses.definition: to include or involve someone in a particular activity, decision, or plan

examples.x: Don't forget to count in Jane for the brainstorming session; she has valuable insights.
The manager decided to count in the entire department for the strategic planning meeting.

senses.definition: to be involved in or conduct activities related to a particular kind of business, commodity, or trade

examples.x: The antique shop specializes in dealing in rare and valuable collectibles.
As a real estate agent, she primarily deals in residential properties in the downtown area.
The company is known for dealing in innovative technologies and cutting-edge solutions.
Their family has been dealing in fine wines for generations, offering a diverse collection.

senses.definition: to enthusiastically start doing something without thinking about it

examples.x: Excited about the new project, she decided to dive in and contribute her expertise.
Instead of hesitating, the students were encouraged to dive in and explore the challenging assignment.
The entrepreneur was ready to dive in and launch the startup despite the uncertainties.
Recognizing the importance of the issue, the community members were eager to dive in and address the challenges.
The team decided to dive in and tackle the complex problem head-on, working collaboratively to find solutions.

senses.definition: to engage or involve someone in a particular activity, situation, or conversation

examples.x: The team leader decided to draw in new members for the upcoming project.
The teacher encouraged students to draw in their classmates for a collaborative assignment.
During the meeting, the manager sought to draw in everyone's opinions on the proposed changes.
The community event aimed to draw in residents and encourage participation.
To enhance creativity, the workshop facilitator tried to draw in every participant by encouraging contributions.

senses.definition: to participate in or become involved in a particular activity, conversation, etc.

examples.x: The students were encouraged to engage in extracurricular activities to enhance their overall development.
Before making a decision, it's essential to engage in thoughtful discussions to consider all perspectives.
The company decided to engage in a philanthropic initiative to support the local community.
Instead of avoiding the issue, it's better to engage in open communication to resolve conflicts.
Athletes often engage in rigorous training sessions to improve their performance.

senses.definition: to join a group or organization

examples.x: Excited about the cause, he decided to fall in and become an active member of the environmental organization.
Eager to contribute to the team, he decided to fall in and become a member of the project committee.
The club had an open invitation for anyone interested to fall in and participate in their upcoming events.

senses.definition: to be socially fit for or belong within a particular group or environment

examples.x: Moving to a new school can be challenging, but she quickly found a way to fit in with her classmates.
He tried to fit in with the local culture by learning the language and customs.
It took a while for the new employee to fit in with the team, but eventually, they became close-knit.
In high school, everyone wants to fit in and be part of a social group.
Over time, he learned to fit in with the local traditions and lifestyle.

senses.definition: to gently mix one ingredient into another by lifting and turning the mixture with a spatula or spoon

examples.x: The chef instructed the apprentices to fold in the whipped cream into the chocolate mousse.
When baking, it's important to fold in the dry ingredients gradually to create a smooth batter.

senses.definition: to participate in an ongoing activity or opportunity when others are already involved
to engage in an activity or interest as a hobby or pastime

examples.x: Many people go in for gardening as a way to unwind and connect with nature.
He decided to go in for photography, capturing beautiful moments during his travels.
They go in for birdwatching, observing various species in their local park.

senses.definition: to take part in an activity or event that others are already engaged in

examples.x: Despite attending the meeting, he rarely joins in the debates.
We hope more people will join in with our neighborhood watch program.
The team is friendly, and they encourage new members to join in the activities.
At the family gathering, it's essential that everyone feels comfortable enough to join in.

senses.definition: to get involved in something without delay

examples.x: She didn't waste any time; she jumped in and took charge of the emergency situation.
He's the kind of person who loves to jump in and help whenever there's a problem.
When a new project comes up, they're always the first to jump in and get things started.
The volunteers are always ready to jump in and assist during community events.

senses.definition: to get oneself or someone into trouble or difficulty

examples.x: The government landed in a political crisis when it was accused of corruption.

senses.definition: to study a particular subject as one's main field of study at a college or university

examples.x: I am majoring in computer science because I want to be a software engineer.
He plans to major in Computer Science and develop software applications.
They are both majoring in business because they want to start their own companies.
I majored in English at Stanford University.

senses.definition: to choose to participate in something, typically by actively indicating one's willingness or consent to do so

examples.x: During the registration process, you have the option to opt in for email notifications.
To join the loyalty program, customers need to opt in and provide their consent.
Users can opt in to share their location information for a more personalized experience.
Employees can opt in to training programs to enhance their professional skills.
Customers are encouraged to opt in for exclusive offers and discounts.
Participants must opt in to the research study by signing the informed consent form.

senses.definition: to be deeply surrounded by a significant amount of tradition or history

examples.x: The small town is steeped in tradition, celebrating annual festivals passed down through generations.
The museum is steeped in art, showcasing masterpieces from renowned artists.
The historical landmark is steeped in significance, representing a crucial moment in the country's history.
The university is steeped in academic excellence, boasting a long list of renowned scholars and alumni.

senses.definition: to get involved in an argument, discussion, or activity and share one's opinions

examples.x: The CEO wanted to weigh in and offer guidance on the strategic direction of the company.
As the discussion progressed, each team member weighed in with valuable insights.
The professor welcomed students to weigh in with their interpretations of the literary text.
